title: Smoothed race-compound hazard curves
summary: Pit windows are lap ranges where stopping becomes strategically viable, not just a raw tyre-life threshold.
rationale: Pit windows are lap ranges where stopping becomes strategically viable, not just a raw tyre-life threshold. A smoothed empirical hazard by Race, Year, Compound, Stint, TyreLife bin, and RaceProgress bin can capture event-specific strategy timing beyond hand-coded expected-life constants.
implementation_hint: Create out-of-fold target-rate encodings for grouped bins such as Race_Compound_Stint_TyreLifeBin and Year_Race_ProgressBin with Bayesian smoothing toward global and compound-level priors. For test, fit encodings on all train only; avoid using test target surrogates.
expected_effect: Should add a compact prior for optimal stop timing and improve ranking around pit-window boundaries where many rows have similar tyre-life arithmetic.
risk: Random CV can overstate gains; use GroupKFold by Year/Race or at least out-of-fold encodings to avoid leaking race-specific labels.
